quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Midterm Review Quiz 5
quiz.subtitle: Comprehensive Review — Kinematics through Simple Machines
quiz.subject: Physics

questions.question: A motorcycle driver travelling along a highway at $26.0\text{ m/s}$ spots an obstruction ahead and immediately applies the brakes, creating a steady deceleration of $3.80\text{ m/s}^2$. Calculate the final speed of the motorcycle after it has skidded a distance of $45.0\text{ m}$.

A logistics specialist reviews shipping manifests for an international freight container carrying specialized steel beams. The total cargo mass is recorded as $14.8\text{ tons}$. Convert this value into kilograms ($\text{kg}$), given that $1\text{ ton} = 1,000\text{ kg}$.

A skydiver descends through the atmosphere at a perfectly constant terminal velocity because the upward force of air resistance exactly balances the downward force of gravity. Determine whether the net work done on the skydiver by all combined forces during a $50.0\text{ m}$ displacement at terminal velocity is positive, negative, or zero.
questions.explanation: At terminal velocity, net force = 0 (forces are balanced). $W_{net} = F_{net} \times d = 0 \times 50.0 = 0$.

questions.question: An experimental toy launcher uses a compressed internal coil spring to project small items. The spring has a stiffness constant of $480\text{ N/m}$ and is compressed by $0.0750\text{ m}$ from its relaxed state. Calculate the elastic strain energy stored ready for launch.

A $2.40\text{ kg}$ block is pushed along a frictionless horizontal workshop bench by a constant horizontal force of $18.5\text{ N}$. The block starts completely from rest. Use energy principles to determine its kinetic energy after the force has acted over a distance of $3.20\text{ m}$.
questions.explanation: $W = F \times d = 18.5 \times 3.20 = 59.2\text{ J}$. On a frictionless surface, all work becomes KE.

questions.question: A $0.0580\text{ kg}$ tennis ball strikes a vertical training wall horizontally at $32.0\text{ m/s}$ and bounces directly backward at $24.0\text{ m/s}$. Find the magnitude of the total impulse delivered to the ball by the wall during the collision.

A heavy-duty electric winch pulls a $1,400\text{ kg}$ vehicle out of a ditch by pulling it up a steady incline at constant speed. The winch performs $2.52 \times 10^5\text{ J}$ of useful work over exactly $42.0\text{ s}$. Calculate the average power output of the winch system.

A workshop air compressor consumes $1,500\text{ J}$ of total electrical energy during an operation cycle. The machine's internal friction converts $420\text{ J}$ of that input energy into useless thermal energy. Calculate the efficiency percentage of the compressor system.

Two ice skaters stand facing each other at rest on a frictionless ice rink. Skater 1 ($72.0\text{ kg}$) and Skater 2 ($48.0\text{ kg}$) push off against each other, causing Skater 1 to move due west at $2.60\text{ m/s}$. Calculate the velocity of Skater 2 immediately after the push.

A warehouse team uses a heavy-duty hydraulic jack system with an ideal mechanical advantage (IMA) of $24.0$. A worker applies an effort force of $150\text{ N}$ to lift a load of $2,880\text{ N}$. Calculate the actual mechanical advantage (AMA) of the jack system.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You're well-prepared across all units!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review the topics where you made errors.
scoreMessages.50: Keep studying! Focus on impulse during rebounds, net work at terminal velocity, and efficiency.
scoreMessages.0: Significant review needed. Revisit kinematics, energy, momentum, and simple machines.
